summary: The changelog itself mostly satisfies the original acceptance criteria, but the expanded workflow-fix scope is only partially reflected. The branch leaves stale `codex exec review` references in the review skill/changelog and the story Build note no longer matches the actual four-file diff.

findings.title: Review skill still advertises obsolete subcommand
findings.claim: The added instruction says not to use the `codex exec review` subcommand, but the same skill still describes itself as running review via `codex exec review` in its metadata/intro. That undercuts the scope-expanded fix because future invocations may still be guided by the obsolete command wording.
findings.suggestion: Update the skill description and intro text to describe the corrected `codex exec -s read-only ... --output-schema ...` handoff, or use command-neutral wording consistently.

findings.title: Changelog preserves the known-bad review command
findings.claim: The changelog entry says the review skill uses `codex exec review`, while the expanded scope explicitly includes correcting the `/review` Codex command and the skill now forbids that subcommand. This leaves the human-readable workflow record pointing future adopters at the command this branch is supposed to retire.
findings.suggestion: Change the changelog wording to reference the corrected read-only `codex exec` structured-output handoff, and mention the strict findings schema without tying it to `codex exec review`.

findings.title: Build note omits expanded-scope files
findings.claim: The Build note records a `git diff --stat main...HEAD` with only `CHANGELOG.md` and `reviews/add-changelog.md`, but the actual required diff also changes `.claude/skills/review/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/review/finding-schema.json`. That contradicts the story's expanded scope and weakens the audit trail the reviewer is told to use.
findings.suggestion: Regenerate the Build note's AC/file map and diff stat after the workflow-fix commits so it reflects all four changed files and the expanded scope.
